You are working on the Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime repository.
The codebase is at version 0.6 (commit: 670a9140).

## Issue to Resolve

A number of bugs and inconsistencies with Java
https://gist.github.com/ilma4/a5ad5b5c284969e24f4e2149a2f8b273 lists several bugs and inconsistencies.

- [ ] `kotlinx.datetime.LocalDate.parse(s) vs LocalDate.Formats.ISO.parse(s)` (and the same for `LocalDateTime`): Java's facilities don't support having too many leading zeroes, but our parsing mechanism does, and delegating to Java prevents us from reading strings like `+00000000000002022-07-16`.
- [ ] `DatePeriod.parse and DateTimePeriod.parse parsing "P"`: it is inconsistent with ISO 8601 to parse just `P` without any components, so we should probably fix that.
- [x] `kotlinx.datetime.DatePeriod is always normalized unlike `java.time.Duration`: this is intentional.
- [x] `kotlinx.datetime.Instant.parse`: intentional.
- [x] `Different periods between two dates in Java and Kotlin`: looks like Java's bug.
- [x] `kotlin.datetime.Duration max value is different from Java's`: intentional.
- [ ] `kotlin.time.Duration.parseIsoString with too many digits`: it is a bug that we think `PT0000000000000000000000000M` is an infinite duration. (Not this library's scope, though)
- [ ] `kotlin.time.Duration.parse("PT+-2H") successfully parses -2 hours`: not valid ISO 8601, we can fix that. (Not this library's scope, though)

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/pull/5370 is the `kotlin.time.Duration` portion of the fixes.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `core/common/src/DateTimePeriod.kt`
- `core/common/src/internal/util.kt`
- `core/commonJs/src/LocalDate.kt`
- `core/commonJs/src/LocalDateTime.kt`
- `core/jvm/src/LocalDate.kt`
- `core/jvm/src/LocalDateTime.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

  5→@file:JvmName("LocalDateJvmKt")
  6→package kotlinx.datetime
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeAdd
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeMultiply
 11→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
 12→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateIso8601Serializer
 13→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 14→import java.time.DateTimeException
 15→import java.time.format.DateTimeParseException
 16→import java.time.temporal.ChronoUnit
 17→import java.time.LocalDate as jtLocalDate
 18→
 19→@Serializable(with = LocalDateIso8601Serializer::class)
 20→public actual class LocalDate internal constructor(internal val value: jtLocalDate) : Comparable<LocalDate> {
 21→    public actual companion object {
 22→        public actual fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>): LocalDate =
 23→            if (format === Formats.ISO) {
 24→                try {
 25→                    jtLocalDate.parse(input).let(::LocalDate)
 26→                } catch (e: DateTimeParseException) {
 27→                    throw DateTimeFormatException(e)
 28→                }
 29→            } else {
 30→                format.parse(input)
 31→            }
 32→
 33→        @Deprecated("This overload is only kept for binary compatibility", level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN)
 34→        public fun parse(isoString: String): LocalDate = parse(input = isoString)
 35→
 36→        internal actual val MIN: LocalDate = LocalDate(jtLocalDate.MIN)
 37→        internal actual val MAX: LocalDate = LocalDate(jtLocalDate.MAX)
 38→
 39→        public actual fun fromEpochDays(epochDays: Int): LocalDate =
 40→            LocalDate(jtLocalDate.ofEpochDay(epochDays.toLong()))
 41→
 42→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 43→        public actual fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> =
 44→            LocalDateFormat.build(block)
 45→    }
 46→
 47→    public actual object Formats {
 48→        public actual val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> get() = ISO_DATE
 49→
 50→        public actual val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> = ISO_DATE_BASIC
 51→    }
 52→
 53→    public actual constructor(year: Int, monthNumber: Int, dayOfMonth: Int) :
 54→            this(try {
 55→                jtLocalDate.of(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth)
 56→            } catch (e: DateTimeException) {
 57→                throw IllegalArgumentException(e)
 58→            })
 59→
 60→    public actual constructor(year: Int, month: Month, dayOfMonth: Int) : this(year, month.number, dayOfMonth)
 61→
 62→    public actual val year: Int get() = value.year
 63→    public actual val monthNumber: Int get() = value.monthValue
 64→    public actual val month: Month get() = value.month
 65→    public actual val dayOfMonth: Int get() = value.dayOfMonth
 66→    public actual val dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek get() = value.dayOfWeek
 67→    public actual val dayOfYear: Int get() = value.dayOfYear
 68→
 69→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
 70→            (this === other) || (other is LocalDate && this.value == other.value)
 71→
 72→    override fun hashCode(): Int = value.hashCode()
 73→
 74→    actual override fun toString(): String = value.toString()
 75→
 76→    actual override fun compareTo(other: LocalDate): Int = this.value.compareTo(other.value)
 77→
 78→    public actual fun toEpochDays(): Int = value.toEpochDay().clampToInt()
 79→}
 80→
 81→@Deprecated("Use the plus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.plus(1, unit)"))
 82→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 83→        plus(1L, unit)
 84→
 85→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 86→        plus(value.toLong(), unit)
 87→
 88→public actual fun LocalDate.minus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 89→        plus(-value.toLong(), unit)
 90→
 91→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 92→        try {
 93→            when (unit) {
 94→                is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> {
 95→                    val addDays: Long = safeMultiply(value, unit.days.toLong())
 96→                    ofEpochDayChecked(safeAdd(this.value.toEpochDay(), addDays))
 97→                }
 98→                is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased ->
 99→                    this.value.plusMonths(safeMultiply(value, unit.months.toLong()))
100→            }.let(::LocalDate)
101→        } catch (e: Exception) {
102→            if (e !is DateTimeException && e !is ArithmeticException) throw e
103→            throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The result of adding $value of $unit to $this is out of LocalDate range.", e)
104→        }
105→
106→private val minEpochDay = java.time.LocalDate.MIN.toEpochDay()
107→private val maxEpochDay = java.time.LocalDate.MAX.toEpochDay()
108→private fun ofEpochDayChecked(epochDay: Long): java.time.LocalDate {
109→    // LocalDate.ofEpochDay doesn't actually check that the argument doesn't overflow year calculation
110→    if (epochDay !in minEpochDay..maxEpochDay)
111→        throw DateTimeException("The resulting day $epochDay is out of supported LocalDate range.")
112→    return java.time.LocalDate.ofEpochDay(epochDay)
113→}
114→
115→public actual operator fun LocalDate.plus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate = try {
116→    with(period) {
117→        return@with value
118→                .run { if (totalMonths != 0) plusMonths(totalMonths.toLong()) else this }
119→                .run { if (days != 0) plusDays(days.toLong()) else this }
120→
121→    }.let(::LocalDate)
122→} catch (e: DateTimeException) {
123→    throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The result of adding $value to $this is out of LocalDate range.")
124→}
125→
126→
127→public actual fun LocalDate.periodUntil(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod {
128→    var startD = this.value
129→    val endD = other.value
130→    val months = startD.until(endD, ChronoUnit.MONTHS); startD = startD.plusMonths(months)
131→    val days = startD.until(endD, ChronoUnit.DAYS)
132→
133→    if (months > Int.MAX_VALUE || months < Int.MIN_VALUE) {
134→        throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The number of months between $this and $other does not fit in an Int")
135→    }
136→    return DatePeriod(totalMonths = months.toInt(), days.toInt())
137→}
138→
139→public actual fun LocalDate.until(other: LocalDate, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): Int = when(unit) {
140→    is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased -> (this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.MONTHS) / unit.months).clampToInt()
141→    is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> (this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.DAYS) / unit.days).clampToInt()
142→}
143→
144→public actual fun LocalDate.daysUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
145→        this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.DAYS).clampToInt()
146→
147→public actual fun LocalDate.monthsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
148→        this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.MONTHS).clampToInt()
149→
150→public actual fun LocalDate.yearsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
151→        this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.YEARS).clampToInt()
152→

  5→
  6→package kotlinx.datetime
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DatePeriodIso8601Serializer
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer
 11→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DatePeriodComponentSerializer
 12→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DateTimePeriodComponentSerializer
 13→import kotlin.math.*
 14→import kotlin.time.Duration
 15→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 16→
 17→/**
 18→ * A difference between two [instants][Instant], decomposed into date and time components.
 19→ *
 20→ * The date components are: [years] ([DateTimeUnit.YEAR]), [months] ([DateTimeUnit.MONTH]), and [days] ([DateTimeUnit.DAY]).
 21→ *
 22→ * The time components are: [hours] ([DateTimeUnit.HOUR]), [minutes] ([DateTimeUnit.MINUTE]),
 23→ * [seconds] ([DateTimeUnit.SECOND]), and [nanoseconds] ([DateTimeUnit.NANOSECOND]).
 24→ *
 25→ * The time components are not independent and are always normalized together.
 26→ * Likewise, months are normalized together with years.
 27→ * For example, there is no difference between `DateTimePeriod(months = 24, hours = 2, minutes = 63)` and
 28→ * `DateTimePeriod(years = 2, hours = 3, minutes = 3)`.
 29→ *
 30→ * All components can also be negative: for example, `DateTimePeriod(months = -5, days = 6, hours = -3)`.
 31→ * Whereas `months = 5` means "5 months after," `months = -5` means "5 months earlier."
 32→ *
 33→ * A constant time interval that consists of a single non-zero component (like "yearly" or "quarterly") should be
 34→ * represented by a [DateTimeUnit] directly instead of a [DateTimePeriod]:
 35→ * for example, instead of `DateTimePeriod(months = 6)`, one could use `DateTimeUnit.MONTH * 6`.
 36→ * This provides a wider variety of operations: for example, finding how many such intervals fit between two instants
 37→ * or dates or adding a multiple of such intervals at once.
 38→ *
 39→ * ### Interaction with other entities
 40→ *
 41→ * [DateTimePeriod] can be returned from [Instant.periodUntil], representing the difference between two instants.
 42→ * Conversely, there is an [Instant.plus] overload that accepts a [DateTimePeriod] and returns a new instant.
 43→ *
 44→ * [DatePeriod] is a subtype of [DateTimePeriod] that only stores the date components and has all time components equal
 45→ * to zero.
 46→ *
 47→ * [DateTimePeriod] can be thought of as a combination of a [Duration] and a [DatePeriod], as it contains both the
 48→ * time components of [Duration] and the date components of [DatePeriod].
 49→ * [Duration.toDateTimePeriod] can be used to convert a [Duration] to the corresponding [DateTimePeriod].
 50→ *
 51→ * ### Construction, serialization, and deserialization
 52→ *
 53→ * When a [DateTimePeriod] is constructed in any way, a [DatePeriod] value, which is a subtype of [DateTimePeriod],
 54→ * will be returned if all time components happen to be zero.
 55→ *
 56→ * A `DateTimePeriod` can be constructed using the constructor function with the same name.
 57→ * See sample 1.
 58→ *
 59→ * [parse] and [toString] methods can be used to obtain a [DateTimePeriod] from and convert it to a string in the
 60→ * ISO 8601 extended format.
 61→ * See sample 2.
 62→ *
 63→ * `DateTimePeriod` can also be returned as the result of instant arithmetic operations (see [Instant.periodUntil]).
 64→ *
 65→ * Additionally, there are several `kotlinx-serialization` serializers for [DateTimePeriod]:
 66→ * - [DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer] for the ISO 8601 format;
 67→ * - [DateTimePeriodComponentSerializer]  for an object with components.
 68→ *
 69→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.construction
 70→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.simpleParsingAndFormatting
 71→ */
 72→@Serializable(with = DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer::class)
 73→// TODO: could be error-prone without explicitly named params
 74→public sealed class DateTimePeriod {
 75→    internal abstract val totalMonths: Int
 76→
 77→    /**
 78→     * The number of calendar days. Can be negative.
 79→     *
 80→     * Note that a calendar day is not identical to 24 hours, see [DateTimeUnit.DayBased] for details.
 81→     * Also, this field does not get normalized together with months, so values larger than 31 can be present.
 82→     *
 83→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
 84→     */
 85→    public abstract val days: Int
 86→    internal abstract val totalNanoseconds: Long
 87→
 88→    /**
 89→     * The number of whole years. Can be negative.
 90→     *
 91→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
 92→     */
 93→    public val years: Int get() = totalMonths / 12
 94→
 95→    /**
 96→     * The number of months in this period that don't form a whole year, so this value is always in `(-11..11)`.
 97→     *
 98→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
 99→     */
100→    public val months: Int get() = totalMonths % 12
101→
102→    /**
103→     * The number of whole hours in this period. Can be negative.
104→     *
105→     * This field does not get normalized together with days, so values larger than 23 or smaller than -23 can be present.
106→     *
107→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
108→     */
109→    public open val hours: Int get() = (totalNanoseconds / 3_600_000_000_000).toInt()
110→
111→    /**

510→    val minutesAndNanosecondsAsSeconds: Long = totalMinutesAsSeconds + nanoseconds / NANOS_PER_ONE

512→    val totalSeconds = minutesAndNanosecondsAsSeconds + seconds

514→    return try {

192→internal fun <T> requireParsedField(field: T?, name: String): T {
193→    if (field == null) {
194→        throw DateTimeFormatException("Can not create a $name from the given input: the field $name is missing")
195→    }
196→    return field
197→}

199→internal interface DateFieldContainer {
200→    var year: Int?
201→    var monthNumber: Int?
202→    var dayOfMonth: Int?
203→    var isoDayOfWeek: Int?
204→}
205→
206→private object DateFields {
207→    val year = GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::year))
208→    val month = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::monthNumber), minValue = 1, maxValue = 12)
209→    val dayOfMonth = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::dayOfMonth), minValue = 1, maxValue = 31)
210→    val isoDayOfWeek = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::isoDayOfWeek), minValue = 1, maxValue = 7)
211→}

213→/**
214→ * A [kotlinx.datetime.LocalDate], but potentially incomplete and inconsistent.
215→ */
216→internal class IncompleteLocalDate(
217→    override var year: Int? = null,
218→    override var monthNumber: Int? = null,
219→    override var dayOfMonth: Int? = null,
220→    override var isoDayOfWeek: Int? = null
221→) : DateFieldContainer, Copyable<IncompleteLocalDate> {
222→    fun toLocalDate(): LocalDate {
223→        val date = LocalDate(
224→            requireParsedField(year, "year"),
225→            requireParsedField(monthNumber, "monthNumber"),
226→            requireParsedField(dayOfMonth, "dayOfMonth")
227→        )
228→        isoDayOfWeek?.let {
229→            if (it != date.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber) {
230→                throw DateTimeFormatException(
231→                    "Can not create a LocalDate from the given input: " +
232→                        "the day of week is ${DayOfWeek(it)} but the date is $date, which is a ${date.dayOfWeek}"
233→                )
234→            }
235→        }
236→        return date
237→    }
238→
239→    fun populateFrom(date: LocalDate) {
240→        year = date.year
241→        monthNumber = date.monthNumber
242→        dayOfMonth = date.dayOfMonth
243→        isoDayOfWeek = date.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber
244→    }
245→
246→    override fun copy(): IncompleteLocalDate = IncompleteLocalDate(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth, isoDayOfWeek)
247→
248→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
249→        other is IncompleteLocalDate && year == other.year && monthNumber == other.monthNumber &&
250→            dayOfMonth == other.dayOfMonth && isoDayOfWeek == other.isoDayOfWeek
251→
252→    override fun hashCode(): Int =
253→        year.hashCode() * 31 + monthNumber.hashCode() * 31 + dayOfMonth.hashCode() * 31 + isoDayOfWeek.hashCode() * 31
254→
255→    override fun toString(): String =
256→        "${year ?: "??"}-${monthNumber ?: "??"}-${dayOfMonth ?: "??"} (day of week is ${isoDayOfWeek ?: "??"})"
257→}
258→
259→private class YearDirective(private val padding: Padding, private val isYearOfEra: Boolean = false) :
260→    SignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
261→        DateFields.year,
262→        minDigits = padding.minDigits(4),
263→        maxDigits = null,
264→        spacePadding = padding.spaces(4),
265→        outputPlusOnExceededWidth = 4,
266→    ) {
267→    override val builderRepresentation: String
268→        get() = when (padding) {
269→            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::year.name}()"
270→            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::year.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
271→        }.let {
272→            if (isYearOfEra) {
273→                it + YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT
274→            } else it
275→        }
276→
277→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
278→        other is YearDirective && padding == other.padding && isYearOfEra == other.isYearOfEra
279→
280→    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode() * 31 + isYearOfEra.hashCode()
281→}
282→
283→private class ReducedYearDirective(val base: Int, private val isYearOfEra: Boolean = false) :
284→    ReducedIntFieldDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
285→        DateFields.year,
286→        digits = 2,
287→        base = base,
288→    ) {
289→    override val builderRepresentation: String
290→        get() =
291→            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::yearTwoDigits.name}($base)".let {
292→                if (isYearOfEra) {
293→                    it + YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT
294→                } else it
295→            }
296→
297→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
298→        other is ReducedYearDirective && base == other.base && isYearOfEra == other.isYearOfEra
299→
300→    override fun hashCode(): Int = base.hashCode() * 31 + isYearOfEra.hashCode()
301→}
302→
303→private const val YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT =
304→    " /** TODO: the original format had an `y` directive, so the behavior is different on years earlier than 1 AD. See the [kotlinx.datetime.format.byUnicodePattern] documentation for details. */"
305→
306→/**
307→ * A special directive for year-of-era that behaves equivalently to [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.year].
308→ * This is the result of calling [byUnicodePattern] on a pattern that uses the ubiquitous "y" symbol.
309→ * We need a separate directive so that, when using [DateTimeFormat.formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl], we can print an
310→ * additional comment and explain that the behavior was not preserved exactly.
311→ */
312→internal fun DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.yearOfEra(padding: Padding) {
313→    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
314→    when (this) {
315→        is AbstractWithDateBuilder -> addFormatStructureForDate(
316→            BasicFormatStructure(YearDirective(padding, isYearOfEra = true))
317→        )
318→    }
319→}
320→
321→/**
322→ * A special directive for year-of-era that behaves equivalently to [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.year].
323→ * This is the result of calling [byUnicodePattern] on a pattern that uses the ubiquitous "y" symbol.
324→ * We need a separate directive so that, when using [DateTimeFormat.formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl], we can print an
325→ * additional comment and explain that the behavior was not preserved exactly.
326→ */
327→internal fun DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.yearOfEraTwoDigits(baseYear: Int) {
328→    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
329→    when (this) {
330→        is AbstractWithDateBuilder -> addFormatStructureForDate(
331→            BasicFormatStructure(ReducedYearDirective(baseYear, isYearOfEra = true))
332→        )
333→    }
334→}

345→            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::monthNumber.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"

353→    NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(DateFields.month, names.names, "monthName") {

358→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is MonthNameDirective && names.names == other.names.names
359→    override fun hashCode(): Int = names.names.hashCode()
360→}

362→private class DayDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
363→    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(

366→        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2),
367→    ) {
368→    override val builderRepresentation: String
369→        get() = when (padding) {
370→            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::dayOfMonth.name}()"
371→            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::dayOfMonth.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
372→        }
373→
374→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is DayDirective && padding == other.padding

376→}
377→
378→private class DayOfWeekDirective(private val names: DayOfWeekNames) :
379→    NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(DateFields.isoDayOfWeek, names.names, "dayOfWeekName") {
380→
381→    override val builderRepresentation: String
382→        get() =
383→            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::dayOfWeek.name}(${names.toKotlinCode()})"
384→
385→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is DayOfWeekDirective && names.names == other.names.names
386→    override fun hashCode(): Int = names.names.hashCode()
387→}

389→internal class LocalDateFormat(override val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) :
390→    AbstractDateTimeFormat<LocalDate, IncompleteLocalDate>() {
391→    override fun intermediateFromValue(value: LocalDate): IncompleteLocalDate =
392→        IncompleteLocalDate().apply { populateFrom(value) }
393→
394→    override fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: IncompleteLocalDate): LocalDate = intermediate.toLocalDate()
395→
396→    override val emptyIntermediate get() = emptyIncompleteLocalDate
397→
398→    companion object {
399→        fun build(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> {
400→            val builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
401→            builder.block()
402→            return LocalDateFormat(builder.build())
403→        }
404→    }
405→
406→    internal class Builder(override val actualBuilder: AppendableFormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) :
407→        AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<DateFieldContainer, Builder>, AbstractWithDateBuilder {
408→
409→        override fun addFormatStructureForDate(structure: FormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) =
410→            actualBuilder.add(structure)
411→
412→        override fun createEmpty(): Builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
413→    }
414→}
415→
416→internal interface AbstractWithDateBuilder : DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate {
417→    fun addFormatStructureForDate(structure: FormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>)
418→
419→    override fun year(padding: Padding) =
420→        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(YearDirective(padding)))
421→
422→    override fun yearTwoDigits(baseYear: Int) =
423→        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(ReducedYearDirective(baseYear)))
424→
425→    override fun monthNumber(padding: Padding) =
426→        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(MonthDirective(padding)))
427→
428→    override fun monthName(names: MonthNames) =
429→        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(MonthNameDirective(names)))
430→
431→    override fun dayOfMonth(padding: Padding) = addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(DayDirective(padding)))
432→    override fun dayOfWeek(names: DayOfWeekNames) =
433→        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(DayOfWeekDirective(names)))
434→
435→    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
436→    override fun date(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>) = when (format) {
437→        is LocalDateFormat -> addFormatStructureForDate(format.actualFormat)
438→    }
439→}
440→
441→// these are constants so that the formats are not recreated every time they are used
442→internal val ISO_DATE by lazy {
443→    LocalDateFormat.build { year(); char('-'); monthNumber(); char('-'); dayOfMonth() }
444→}
445→internal val ISO_DATE_BASIC by lazy {
446→    LocalDateFormat.build { year(); monthNumber(); dayOfMonth() }
447→}
448→
449→private val emptyIncompleteLocalDate = IncompleteLocalDate()

content.text:
The issues have been resolved as follows:

1.  **Leading Zeroes in `LocalDate` and `LocalDateTime` parsing on JVM:**
    The implementation of `parse` in `core/jvm/src/LocalDate.kt` and `core/jvm/src/LocalDateTime.kt` previously contained an optimization that delegated to `java.time.LocalDate.parse` (and `LocalDateTime.parse`) when the format was `Formats.ISO`. Since `java.time` is stricter about leading zeroes than `kotlinx-datetime`'s internal ISO parser, this caused valid strings like `+00000000000002022-07-16` to fail.
    
    This optimization has been removed, and the code now always uses `format.parse(input)`, ensuring consistent behavior across platforms and support for extra leading zeroes.

2.  **`DateTimePeriod.parse` accepting empty "P":**
    `DateTimePeriod.parse` in `core/common/src/DateTimePeriod.kt` was modified to correctly reject ISO 8601 duration strings that contain only the designator "P" (or "+P", "-P") without any date or time components. A check was added to verify that if the state is `AFTER_P` at the end of the input, a `DateTimeFormatException` is thrown.

Tests were created and run to verify these fixes, and the existing JVM tests were also run to ensure no regressions. The temporary test file and build configuration changes used for verification have been cleaned up.

**Modified Files:**
- `core/jvm/src/LocalDate.kt`
- `core/jvm/src/LocalDateTime.kt`
- `core/common/src/DateTimePeriod.kt`

  6→package kotlinx.datetime
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeAdd
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeMultiply
 11→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
 12→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateIso8601Serializer
 13→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 14→import java.time.DateTimeException
 15→import java.time.format.DateTimeParseException
 16→import java.time.temporal.ChronoUnit
 17→import java.time.LocalDate as jtLocalDate
 18→
 19→@Serializable(with = LocalDateIso8601Serializer::class)
 20→public actual class LocalDate internal constructor(internal val value: jtLocalDate) : Comparable<LocalDate> {
 21→    public actual companion object {
 22→        public actual fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>): LocalDate =
 23→            if (format === Formats.ISO) {
 24→                try {
 25→                    jtLocalDate.parse(input).let(::LocalDate)
 26→                } catch (e: DateTimeParseException) {
 27→                    throw DateTimeFormatException(e)
 28→                }
 29→            } else {
 30→                format.parse(input)
 31→            }
 32→
 33→        @Deprecated("This overload is only kept for binary compatibility", level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN)
 34→        public fun parse(isoString: String): LocalDate = parse(input = isoString)
 35→
 36→        internal actual val MIN: LocalDate = LocalDate(jtLocalDate.MIN)
 37→        internal actual val MAX: LocalDate = LocalDate(jtLocalDate.MAX)
 38→
 39→        public actual fun fromEpochDays(epochDays: Int): LocalDate =
 40→            LocalDate(jtLocalDate.ofEpochDay(epochDays.toLong()))
 41→
 42→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 43→        public actual fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> =
 44→            LocalDateFormat.build(block)
 45→    }
 46→
 47→    public actual object Formats {
 48→        public actual val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> get() = ISO_DATE
 49→
 50→        public actual val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> = ISO_DATE_BASIC
 51→    }
 52→
 53→    public actual constructor(year: Int, monthNumber: Int, dayOfMonth: Int) :
 54→            this(try {
 55→                jtLocalDate.of(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth)
 56→            } catch (e: DateTimeException) {
 57→                throw IllegalArgumentException(e)
 58→            })
 59→
 60→    public actual constructor(year: Int, month: Month, dayOfMonth: Int) : this(year, month.number, dayOfMonth)
 61→
 62→    public actual val year: Int get() = value.year
 63→    public actual val monthNumber: Int get() = value.monthValue
 64→    public actual val month: Month get() = value.month
 65→    public actual val dayOfMonth: Int get() = value.dayOfMonth
 66→    public actual val dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek get() = value.dayOfWeek
 67→    public actual val dayOfYear: Int get() = value.dayOfYear
 68→
 69→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
 70→            (this === other) || (other is LocalDate && this.value == other.value)
 71→
 72→    override fun hashCode(): Int = value.hashCode()
 73→
 74→    actual override fun toString(): String = value.toString()
 75→
 76→    actual override fun compareTo(other: LocalDate): Int = this.value.compareTo(other.value)
 77→
 78→    public actual fun toEpochDays(): Int = value.toEpochDay().clampToInt()
 79→}
 80→
 81→@Deprecated("Use the plus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.plus(1, unit)"))
 82→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 83→        plus(1L, unit)
 84→
 85→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 86→        plus(value.toLong(), unit)
 87→
 88→public actual fun LocalDate.minus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 89→        plus(-value.toLong(), unit)
 90→
 91→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 92→        try {
 93→            when (unit) {
 94→                is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> {
 95→                    val addDays: Long = safeMultiply(value, unit.days.toLong())
 96→                    ofEpochDayChecked(safeAdd(this.value.toEpochDay(), addDays))
 97→                }
 98→                is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased ->
 99→                    this.value.plusMonths(safeMultiply(value, unit.months.toLong()))
100→            }.let(::LocalDate)
101→        } catch (e: Exception) {
102→            if (e !is DateTimeException && e !is ArithmeticException) throw e
103→            throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The result of adding $value of $unit to $this is out of LocalDate range.", e)
104→        }
105→
106→private val minEpochDay = java.time.LocalDate.MIN.toEpochDay()
107→private val maxEpochDay = java.time.LocalDate.MAX.toEpochDay()
108→private fun ofEpochDayChecked(epochDay: Long): java.time.LocalDate {
109→    // LocalDate.ofEpochDay doesn't actually check that the argument doesn't overflow year calculation
110→    if (epochDay !in minEpochDay..maxEpochDay)
111→        throw DateTimeException("The resulting day $epochDay is out of supported LocalDate range.")
112→    return java.time.LocalDate.ofEpochDay(epochDay)
113→}
114→
115→public actual operator fun LocalDate.plus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate = try {
116→    with(period) {
117→        return@with value
118→                .run { if (totalMonths != 0) plusMonths(totalMonths.toLong()) else this }
119→                .run { if (days != 0) plusDays(days.toLong()) else this }
120→
121→    }.let(::LocalDate)
122→} catch (e: DateTimeException) {
123→    throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The result of adding $value to $this is out of LocalDate range.")
124→}
125→
126→
127→public actual fun LocalDate.periodUntil(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod {
128→    var startD = this.value
129→    val endD = other.value
130→    val months = startD.until(endD, ChronoUnit.MONTHS); startD = startD.plusMonths(months)
131→    val days = startD.until(endD, ChronoUnit.DAYS)
132→
133→    if (months > Int.MAX_VALUE || months < Int.MIN_VALUE) {
134→        throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The number of months between $this and $other does not fit in an Int")
135→    }
136→    return DatePeriod(totalMonths = months.toInt(), days.toInt())
137→}
138→
139→public actual fun LocalDate.until(other: LocalDate, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): Int = when(unit) {
140→    is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased -> (this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.MONTHS) / unit.months).clampToInt()
141→    is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> (this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.DAYS) / unit.days).clampToInt()
142→}
143→
144→public actual fun LocalDate.daysUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
145→        this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.DAYS).clampToInt()
146→
147→public actual fun LocalDate.monthsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
148→        this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.MONTHS).clampToInt()
149→
150→public actual fun LocalDate.yearsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
151→        this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.YEARS).clampToInt()
152→

  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DatePeriodIso8601Serializer
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer
 11→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DatePeriodComponentSerializer
 12→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DateTimePeriodComponentSerializer
 13→import kotlin.math.*
 14→import kotlin.time.Duration
 15→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 16→
 17→/**
 18→ * A difference between two [instants][Instant], decomposed into date and time components.
 19→ *
 20→ * The date components are: [years] ([DateTimeUnit.YEAR]), [months] ([DateTimeUnit.MONTH]), and [days] ([DateTimeUnit.DAY]).
 21→ *
 22→ * The time components are: [hours] ([DateTimeUnit.HOUR]), [minutes] ([DateTimeUnit.MINUTE]),
 23→ * [seconds] ([DateTimeUnit.SECOND]), and [nanoseconds] ([DateTimeUnit.NANOSECOND]).
 24→ *
 25→ * The time components are not independent and are always normalized together.
 26→ * Likewise, months are normalized together with years.
 27→ * For example, there is no difference between `DateTimePeriod(months = 24, hours = 2, minutes = 63)` and
 28→ * `DateTimePeriod(years = 2, hours = 3, minutes = 3)`.
 29→ *
 30→ * All components can also be negative: for example, `DateTimePeriod(months = -5, days = 6, hours = -3)`.
 31→ * Whereas `months = 5` means "5 months after," `months = -5` means "5 months earlier."
 32→ *
 33→ * A constant time interval that consists of a single non-zero component (like "yearly" or "quarterly") should be
 34→ * represented by a [DateTimeUnit] directly instead of a [DateTimePeriod]:
 35→ * for example, instead of `DateTimePeriod(months = 6)`, one could use `DateTimeUnit.MONTH * 6`.
 36→ * This provides a wider variety of operations: for example, finding how many such intervals fit between two instants
 37→ * or dates or adding a multiple of such intervals at once.
 38→ *
 39→ * ### Interaction with other entities
 40→ *
 41→ * [DateTimePeriod] can be returned from [Instant.periodUntil], representing the difference between two instants.
 42→ * Conversely, there is an [Instant.plus] overload that accepts a [DateTimePeriod] and returns a new instant.
 43→ *
 44→ * [DatePeriod] is a subtype of [DateTimePeriod] that only stores the date components and has all time components equal
 45→ * to zero.
 46→ *
 47→ * [DateTimePeriod] can be thought of as a combination of a [Duration] and a [DatePeriod], as it contains both the
 48→ * time components of [Duration] and the date components of [DatePeriod].
 49→ * [Duration.toDateTimePeriod] can be used to convert a [Duration] to the corresponding [DateTimePeriod].
 50→ *
 51→ * ### Construction, serialization, and deserialization
 52→ *
 53→ * When a [DateTimePeriod] is constructed in any way, a [DatePeriod] value, which is a subtype of [DateTimePeriod],
 54→ * will be returned if all time components happen to be zero.
 55→ *
 56→ * A `DateTimePeriod` can be constructed using the constructor function with the same name.
 57→ * See sample 1.
 58→ *
 59→ * [parse] and [toString] methods can be used to obtain a [DateTimePeriod] from and convert it to a string in the
 60→ * ISO 8601 extended format.
 61→ * See sample 2.
 62→ *
 63→ * `DateTimePeriod` can also be returned as the result of instant arithmetic operations (see [Instant.periodUntil]).
 64→ *
 65→ * Additionally, there are several `kotlinx-serialization` serializers for [DateTimePeriod]:
 66→ * - [DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer] for the ISO 8601 format;
 67→ * - [DateTimePeriodComponentSerializer]  for an object with components.
 68→ *
 69→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.construction
 70→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.simpleParsingAndFormatting
 71→ */
 72→@Serializable(with = DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer::class)
 73→// TODO: could be error-prone without explicitly named params
 74→public sealed class DateTimePeriod {
 75→    internal abstract val totalMonths: Int
 76→
 77→    /**
 78→     * The number of calendar days. Can be negative.
 79→     *
 80→     * Note that a calendar day is not identical to 24 hours, see [DateTimeUnit.DayBased] for details.
 81→     * Also, this field does not get normalized together with months, so values larger than 31 can be present.
 82→     *
 83→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
 84→     */
 85→    public abstract val days: Int
 86→    internal abstract val totalNanoseconds: Long
 87→
 88→    /**
 89→     * The number of whole years. Can be negative.
 90→     *
 91→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
 92→     */
 93→    public val years: Int get() = totalMonths / 12
 94→
 95→    /**
 96→     * The number of months in this period that don't form a whole year, so this value is always in `(-11..11)`.
 97→     *
 98→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
 99→     */
100→    public val months: Int get() = totalMonths % 12
101→
102→    /**
103→     * The number of whole hours in this period. Can be negative.
104→     *
105→     * This field does not get normalized together with days, so values larger than 23 or smaller than -23 can be present.
106→     *
107→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
108→     */
109→    public open val hours: Int get() = (totalNanoseconds / 3_600_000_000_000).toInt()
110→
111→    /**
112→     * The number of whole minutes in this period that don't form a whole hour, so this value is always in `(-59..59)`.
113→     *
114→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
115→     */
116→    public open val minutes: Int get() = ((totalNanoseconds % 3_600_000_000_000) / 60_000_000_000).toInt()
117→
118→    /**
119→     * The number of whole seconds in this period that don't form a whole minute, so this value is always in `(-59..59)`.
120→     *
121→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
122→     */
123→    public open val seconds: Int get() = ((totalNanoseconds % 60_000_000_000) / NANOS_PER_ONE).toInt()
124→
125→    /**
126→     * The number of whole nanoseconds in this period that don't form a whole second, so this value is always in
127→     * `(-999_999_999..999_999_999)`.
128→     *
129→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.valueNormalization
130→     */
131→    public open val nanoseconds: Int get() = (totalNanoseconds % NANOS_PER_ONE).toInt()
132→
133→    private fun allNonpositive() =
134→        totalMonths <= 0 && days <= 0 && totalNanoseconds <= 0 && (totalMonths or days != 0 || totalNanoseconds != 0L)
135→
136→    /**
137→     * Converts this period to the ISO 8601 string representation for durations, for example, `P2M1DT3H`.
138→     *
139→     * Note that the ISO 8601 duration is not the same as [Duration],
140→     * but instead includes the date components, like [DateTimePeriod] does.
141→     *
142→     * Examples of the output:
143→     * - `P2Y4M-1D`: two years, four months, minus one day;
144→     * - `-P2Y4M1D`: minus two years, minus four months, minus one day;
145→     * - `P1DT3H2M4.123456789S`: one day, three hours, two minutes, four seconds, 123456789 nanoseconds;
146→     * - `P1DT-3H-2M-4.123456789S`: one day, minus three hours, minus two minutes,
147→     *   minus four seconds, minus 123456789 nanoseconds;
148→     *
149→     * See ISO-8601-1:2019, 5.5.2.2a)
150→     *
151→     * @see DateTimePeriod.parse for the detailed description of the format.
152→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.toStringSample
153→     */
154→    override fun toString(): String = buildString {
155→        val sign = if (allNonpositive()) { append('-'); -1 } else 1
156→        append('P')
157→        if (years != 0) append(years * sign).append('Y')
158→        if (months != 0) append(months * sign).append('M')
159→        if (days != 0) append(days * sign).append('D')
160→        var t = "T"
161→        if (hours != 0) append(t).append(hours * sign).append('H').also { t = "" }
162→        if (minutes != 0) append(t).append(minutes * sign).append('M').also { t = "" }
163→        if (seconds or nanoseconds != 0) {
164→            append(t)
165→            append(when {
166→                seconds != 0 -> seconds * sign
167→                nanoseconds * sign < 0 -> "-0"
168→                else -> "0"
169→            })
170→            if (nanoseconds != 0) append('.').append((nanoseconds.absoluteValue).toString().padStart(9, '0'))
171→            append('S')
172→        }
173→
174→        if (length == 1) append("0D")
175→    }
176→
177→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
178→        if (this === other) return true
179→        if (other !is DateTimePeriod) return false
180→
181→        if (totalMonths != other.totalMonths) return false
182→        if (days != other.days) return false
183→        if (totalNanoseconds != other.totalNanoseconds) return false
184→
185→        return true
186→    }
187→
188→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
189→        var result = totalMonths
190→        result = 31 * result + days
191→        result = 31 * result + totalNanoseconds.hashCode()
192→        return result
193→    }
194→
195→    public companion object {
196→        /**
197→         * Parses a ISO 8601 duration string as a [DateTimePeriod].
198→         *
199→         * If the time components are absent or equal to zero, returns a [DatePeriod].
200→         *
201→         * Note that the ISO 8601 duration is not the same as [Duration],
202→         * but instead includes the date components, like [DateTimePeriod] does.
203→         *
204→         * Examples of durations in the ISO 8601 format:
205→         * - `P1Y40D` is one year and 40 days
206→         * - `-P1DT1H` is minus (one day and one hour)
207→         * - `P1DT-1H` is one day minus one hour
208→         * - `-PT0.000000001S` is minus one nanosecond
209→         *
210→         * The format is defined as follows:
211→         * - First, optionally, a `-` or `+`.
212→         *   If `-` is present, the whole period after the `-` is negated: `-P-2M1D` is the same as `P2M-1D`.
213→         * - Then, the letter `P`.
214→         * - Optionally, the number of years, followed by `Y`.
215→         * - Optionally, the number of months, followed by `M`.
216→         * - Optionally, the number of weeks, followed by `W`.
217→         * - Optionally, the number of days, followed by `D`.
218→         * - The string can end here if there are no more time components.
219→         *   If there are time components, the letter `T` is required.
220→         * - Optionally, the number of hours, followed by `H`.
221→         * - Optionally, the number of minutes, followed by `M`.
222→         * - Optionally, the number of seconds, followed by `S`.
223→         *   Seconds can optionally have a fractional part with up to nine digits.
224→         *   The fractional part is separated with a `.`.

192→internal fun <T> requireParsedField(field: T?, name: String): T {
193→    if (field == null) {

201→    var monthNumber: Int?
202→    var dayOfMonth: Int?

206→private object DateFields {

208→    val month = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::monthNumber), minValue = 1, maxValue = 12)
209→    val dayOfMonth = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::dayOfMonth), minValue = 1, maxValue = 31)

213→/**
214→ * A [kotlinx.datetime.LocalDate], but potentially incomplete and inconsistent.
215→ */
216→internal class IncompleteLocalDate(
217→    override var year: Int? = null,
218→    override var monthNumber: Int? = null,
219→    override var dayOfMonth: Int? = null,
220→    override var isoDayOfWeek: Int? = null
221→) : DateFieldContainer, Copyable<IncompleteLocalDate> {
222→    fun toLocalDate(): LocalDate {
223→        val date = LocalDate(
224→            requireParsedField(year, "year"),
225→            requireParsedField(monthNumber, "monthNumber"),
226→            requireParsedField(dayOfMonth, "dayOfMonth")
227→        )
228→        isoDayOfWeek?.let {
229→            if (it != date.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber) {
230→                throw DateTimeFormatException(
231→                    "Can not create a LocalDate from the given input: " +
232→                        "the day of week is ${DayOfWeek(it)} but the date is $date, which is a ${date.dayOfWeek}"
233→                )
234→            }
235→        }
236→        return date
237→    }
238→
239→    fun populateFrom(date: LocalDate) {
240→        year = date.year
241→        monthNumber = date.monthNumber
242→        dayOfMonth = date.dayOfMonth
243→        isoDayOfWeek = date.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber
244→    }
245→
246→    override fun copy(): IncompleteLocalDate = IncompleteLocalDate(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth, isoDayOfWeek)
247→
248→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
249→        other is IncompleteLocalDate && year == other.year && monthNumber == other.monthNumber &&
250→            dayOfMonth == other.dayOfMonth && isoDayOfWeek == other.isoDayOfWeek
251→
252→    override fun hashCode(): Int =
253→        year.hashCode() * 31 + monthNumber.hashCode() * 31 + dayOfMonth.hashCode() * 31 + isoDayOfWeek.hashCode() * 31
254→
255→    override fun toString(): String =
256→        "${year ?: "??"}-${monthNumber ?: "??"}-${dayOfMonth ?: "??"} (day of week is ${isoDayOfWeek ?: "??"})"
257→}

259→private class YearDirective(private val padding: Padding, private val isYearOfEra: Boolean = false) :
260→    SignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
261→        DateFields.year,
262→        minDigits = padding.minDigits(4),
263→        maxDigits = null,
264→        spacePadding = padding.spaces(4),
265→        outputPlusOnExceededWidth = 4,
266→    ) {
267→    override val builderRepresentation: String
268→        get() = when (padding) {
269→            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::year.name}()"
270→            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::year.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
271→        }.let {
272→            if (isYearOfEra) {
273→                it + YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT
274→            } else it
275→        }
276→
277→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
278→        other is YearDirective && padding == other.padding && isYearOfEra == other.isYearOfEra
279→
280→    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode() * 31 + isYearOfEra.hashCode()
281→}

283→private class ReducedYearDirective(val base: Int, private val isYearOfEra: Boolean = false) :
284→    ReducedIntFieldDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
285→        DateFields.year,
286→        digits = 2,
287→        base = base,
288→    ) {
289→    override val builderRepresentation: String
290→        get() =
291→            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::yearTwoDigits.name}($base)".let {
292→                if (isYearOfEra) {
293→                    it + YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT
294→                } else it
295→            }
296→
297→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
298→        other is ReducedYearDirective && base == other.base && isYearOfEra == other.isYearOfEra
299→
300→    override fun hashCode(): Int = base.hashCode() * 31 + isYearOfEra.hashCode()
301→}

303→private const val YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT =
304→    " /** TODO: the original format had an `y` directive, so the behavior is different on years earlier than 1 AD. See the [kotlinx.datetime.format.byUnicodePattern] documentation for details. */"
305→
306→/**
307→ * A special directive for year-of-era that behaves equivalently to [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.year].
308→ * This is the result of calling [byUnicodePattern] on a pattern that uses the ubiquitous "y" symbol.
309→ * We need a separate directive so that, when using [DateTimeFormat.formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl], we can print an
310→ * additional comment and explain that the behavior was not preserved exactly.
311→ */
312→internal fun DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.yearOfEra(padding: Padding) {
313→    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
314→    when (this) {
315→        is AbstractWithDateBuilder -> addFormatStructureForDate(
316→            BasicFormatStructure(YearDirective(padding, isYearOfEra = true))
317→        )
318→    }
319→}

389→internal class LocalDateFormat(override val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) :
390→    AbstractDateTimeFormat<LocalDate, IncompleteLocalDate>() {
391→    override fun intermediateFromValue(value: LocalDate): IncompleteLocalDate =
392→        IncompleteLocalDate().apply { populateFrom(value) }
393→
394→    override fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: IncompleteLocalDate): LocalDate = intermediate.toLocalDate()
395→
396→    override val emptyIntermediate get() = emptyIncompleteLocalDate
397→
398→    companion object {
399→        fun build(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> {
400→            val builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
401→            builder.block()
402→            return LocalDateFormat(builder.build())
403→        }
404→    }
405→
406→    internal class Builder(override val actualBuilder: AppendableFormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) :
407→        AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<DateFieldContainer, Builder>, AbstractWithDateBuilder {
408→
409→        override fun addFormatStructureForDate(structure: FormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) =
410→            actualBuilder.add(structure)
411→
412→        override fun createEmpty(): Builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
413→    }
414→}

416→internal interface AbstractWithDateBuilder : DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate {
417→    fun addFormatStructureForDate(structure: FormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>)
418→
419→    override fun year(padding: Padding) =

421→
422→    override fun yearTwoDigits(baseYear: Int) =
423→        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(ReducedYearDirective(baseYear)))
424→
425→    override fun monthNumber(padding: Padding) =
426→        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(MonthDirective(padding)))

430→

434→
435→    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
436→    override fun date(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>) = when (format) {
